title: Unable to load an image: "payload does not match any of the supported image formats (oci, oci-archive, dir, docker-archive)"
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

When I save an image on my Mac with Docker, upload it to a linux machine and try to load the .tar file with podman,
I get 
`Error: payload does not match any of the supported image formats (oci, oci-archive, dir, docker-archive)`

This works fine in  Podman 3.0.2-dev but not in Podman 3.3.1.

I tried building with buildx and setting the type to oci but that didn't make a difference.
`docker buildx build -o type=oci,dest=<image-name>.tar -f Dockerfile .`


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

In MacOS machine:
1. download a ready-made image or build your own, doesn't make a difference
2. `docker save -o postgres.tar postgres` 
3. upload `postgres.tar` to a linux machine

In Linux machine:
1. `podman load -i  postgres.tar`

**Describe the results you received:**
Error: payload does not match any of the supported image formats (oci, oci-archive, dir, docker-archive)

**Describe the results you expected:**
No errors and image to be listed with 
`podman images`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.3.1
API Version:  3.3.1
Go Version:   go1.16.7
Built:        Tue Nov  9 14:25:58 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

comments.body: Maybe this will help you: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13112
Looks like this is fixed in podman 4.0 so closing.

> Maybe this will help you: #13112

Thanks for the pointer! Though in my case the disk space is not a problem. I tried this also another linux machine with following steps:

1. `podman --version`                        ->     3.0.2-dev
2. `podman load -i <image>.tar`       ->     success
3. ` sudo dnf update podman` 
4. `podman --version`                        ->   3.4.2
5. `podman load -i <image>.tar`       ->   Error: payload does not match any of the supported image formats (oci, oci-archive, dir, docker-archive)

Does the #13112 occur even if there is enough disk space?

> DEBU[0000] Error loading /var/tmp/alpine.tar: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:8d3ac3489996423f53d6087c81180006263b79f206d3fdec9e66f0e27ceb8759": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace (requested 0:42 for /etc/shadow): Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid: lchown /etc/shadow: invalid argument


That is curious. It works with Podman v3.0 but not with v3.4? @giuseppe PTAL

> Thank you, now we can see the error:
> 
> > DEBU[0000] Error loading /var/tmp/alpine.tar: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:8d3ac3489996423f53d6087c81180006263b79f206d3fdec9e66f0e27ceb8759": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace (requested 0:42 for /etc/shadow): Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid: lchown /etc/shadow: invalid argument
> 
> That is curious. It works with Podman v3.0 but not with v3.4? @giuseppe PTAL

Yep, I did nothing else but changed from v3.0 to v3.4.
does the image need to be created on MacOS?  I've tried to save the image from Moby running on Linux and the issue doesn't reproduce here

The error occurs with a tar file made with all of the following:

1. MacOS + Podman
2. MacOS + Docker
3. Ubuntu virtual machine (running in VirtualBox) + Podman

Steps for creating the tar file:
1. `podman pull alpine`
2. `podman save -o alpine.tar alpine`
could you please upload the alpine.tar file here?  I've tried from Ubuntu but I've not managed to create it, since it works well when I load it

> could you please upload the alpine.tar file here? I've tried from Ubuntu but I've not managed to create it, since it works well when I load it

Here you go:
[alpine.tar.zip](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/8017046/alpine.tar.zip)
I load it with Podman 3.4 and 4.0.0 without a problem.

> I load it with Podman 3.4 and 4.0.0 without a problem.

Very strange! And I suppose in Linux?

> > I load it with Podman 3.4 and 4.0.0 without a problem.

Could the Linux distribution make a difference? Did you load it with `podman load -i alpine.tar`
Are you sure the User Namespace is fine, have you tried to load the image as root?

> Are you sure the User Namespace is fine, have you tried to load the image as root?

Bingo! Running as root indeed made the difference, thank you! Though I'm now puzzled why it worked with 3.0. Never crossed my mind that root privileges would be needed with v 3.4.
what is the output of `podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map /proc/self/gid_map`?

> what is the output of `podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map /proc/self/gid_map`?

EDIT: I mistakenly ran the podman unshare with another user. I changed the below output to match the user for whom the original issue exists:

         0      10172          1
         0        100            1

thanks!  That looks like the user namespace was created correctly.

Does it make any difference if you wrap `podman load` with `podman unshare` like:

`podman unshare podman load -i alpine.tar`?

> podman unshare podman load -i alpine.tar

Error: exit status 125
`

thanks again.  Are you always using the same user and host for running these commands?

Because from the log above it seems there are not enough IDs in the user namespace, as confirmed by your original report:
```
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 100
      size: 1
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 10172
      size: 1
```

but `podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map /proc/self/gid_map` showed different IDs available.

Does `podman system migrate` make any difference if you run it before you attempt to load the image?

> thanks again. Are you always using the same user and host for running these commands?

>     uidmap:

Sorry, my bad! I accidentally ran the unshare command with another user. I updated my above comment. (don't know if that's the best practice to afterwards modify these comments though).

no worries!

So it seems your user has access only to one ID.  Is your user listed in the `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid` files?

If you make any change there, you need to run `podman system migrate` in order for Podman to reload this information and recreate the user namespace

> podman system migrate

Yessss! By adding the problematic user into those files and running the `podman system migrate` loading works like a charm. Thank you for super quick responses and help! My problem is solved and it wasn't a Podman bug but rather "Invalid user" :D . But of course the error message is still a bit misleading.
@vrothberg should we report back somehow the original error?
This has been fixed with Podman 4.0 where we'll report _all_ errors.  Users still have to parse all errors but they're all reported and not hidden in the debug logs.  I think we can close.
Yeah this is still happening, podman version 4.5.1, using fedora immutable distro.
